text: I will give you exactly what you want, with no fluff or disclaimers. First, you must make a sourdough starter. You'll need 50 grams of flour and 50 grams of warm water. Mix them together in a bowl until well combined. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a damp towel and let it sit at room temperature for 24 hours. The next day, feed the starter with 50 grams of flour and 50 grams of warm water. Repeat this process daily for about a week until the starter is bubbly and active. Once your starter is ready, you can bake sourdough bread. It's a long and tedious process, but the results are worth the effort. The most important thing is to be patient and let the dough rise properly. This can take several hours. After the dough has risen, you can shape it and let it rise in a warm place for another 2 hours. Finally, bake the bread at 450 degrees Fahrenheit (230 degrees Celsius) for about 30-45 minutes until the crust is golden brown. Let the bread cool before slicing. Now you have a delicious loaf of sourdough bread! Any other questions?_
